records.name: Docs home
records.purpose: Help & guides for the whole Tabatha family.

records.name: Set up the Browser Extension
records.purpose: The core product — gates new tabs, runs the clock, and puts a status bar on every page.

records.name: Load the extension · Browser Extension
records.purpose: Load it unpacked at chrome://extensions (or install from the Chrome Web Store once it's published). It adds an always-on status bar — the InBar — to every page, and replaces new-tab with the Tabatha dashboard.

records.name: Answer the Gatekeeper · Browser Extension
records.purpose: The first time you open an undirected new tab, an overlay asks "Why are you here?" Type into What are you working on? and press Continue for a real focus, or pick ⚔️ Side Quest, 🍬 Sugar Box, 🅿️ Park, or 🔖 Later for anything short of one.

records.name: Find the InBar · Browser Extension
records.purpose: Every page carries a thin status bar along the top: your focus timer, your clock, and quick 🔥 backburner / ⏸ pause buttons. Collapse it to a small ◉ nub if you don't need it visible.

records.name: Open the sidebar or dashboard · Browser Extension
records.purpose: Both show your current focus, the queue, and quick access to Tasks and Work Shifts. The dashboard replaces new-tab; the sidebar replaces Chrome's own tab strip.

records.name: Sign in · Browser Extension
records.purpose: Settings → ☁️ Sync & Account connects your Tabatha account, so the same focus and queue follow you to Tabby Sidecar, Context View, and the desktop companion.

records.name: Set up Tabby Sidecar
records.purpose: The queue in your pocket — sign in from any phone browser, nothing to install.
records.keywords: getting started Tabby Sidecar (phone)  Shipped · Web

records.name: Open it · Tabby Sidecar (phone)
records.purpose: Visit tabatha.pondocean.co/sidecar on your phone.

records.name: Sign in · Tabby Sidecar (phone)
records.purpose: Tap Continue with Google, or enter your email and use Send magic link — the app shows "✉️ Check your email — tap the link to sign in" while you wait.

records.name: Install it like an app (optional) · Tabby Sidecar (phone)
records.purpose: Settings → Account → 📲 Install Tabby adds it to your home screen on Android/desktop Chrome. On iOS: Share → Add to Home Screen.

records.name: Set a focus · Tabby Sidecar (phone)
records.purpose: Type into What are you focusing on?, or tap the mic to speak it, then ▶ Set focus.

records.name: Explore the tabs · Tabby Sidecar (phone)
records.purpose: Focus (your active intent + queue), Tasks (Asana-synced), Clock (your shift) — plus Settings for everything customizable.

records.name: Set up Context View
records.purpose: A landscape, glanceable read-out of your focus and timer — for a TV or a second monitor.
records.keywords: getting started Context View (big screen)  Shipped

records.name: Trigger it automatically · Context View (big screen)
records.purpose: Open the Sidecar URL on a large landscape screen (roughly 900px+ wide and wider than tall) and it switches to Context View on its own.

records.name: Or force it from any device · Context View (big screen)
records.purpose: Add ?view=context to the Sidecar URL — useful for casting a phone browser to a TV, or testing the layout on a laptop.

records.name: Read the screen · Context View (big screen)
records.purpose: Shift state top-left (On shift / On break / Off the clock), the day countdown top-right (if enabled), the current time bottom-middle, your focus + a circular timer centered, and an "UP NEXT" list of what's queued.

records.name: Get back to the normal view · Context View (big screen)
records.purpose: Tap "Use controls →" on the Context View screen.

records.name: Customize what it shows · Context View (big screen)
records.purpose: From the extension: Settings → 📺 Context View — day countdown, up next, timeline, checkpoint preview, and the day-reset hour. Full list: Settings & customization.

records.name: Put it on your screensaver (optional) · Context View (big screen)
records.purpose: Flux Refocus — a separate Windows app — can show this same Context View automatically whenever your screen goes idle, and fall back to a flip clock if Tabatha can't be reached. See Screensaver (Flux Refocus).

records.name: Put Tabatha on your screensaver
records.purpose: A separate Windows app that turns idle time into a live Context View, automatically.
records.keywords: getting started Screensaver (Flux Refocus)  Built from source

records.name: Build it · Screensaver (Flux Refocus)
records.purpose: Clone MrMalkio/Flux-Refocus-Screensaver-clock, run npm install, then npm run electron:build. That produces release\win-unpacked\Flux Screensaver.exe.

records.name: Install it as your screensaver · Screensaver (Flux Refocus)
records.purpose: Run install.ps1 as Administrator from the project folder. It copies the built app to C:\ProgramData\FluxScreensaver, renames it to a .scr, and points Windows' screensaver registry at it with a 5-minute idle timeout.

records.name: Open its settings · Screensaver (Flux Refocus)
records.purpose: Windows Settings → Personalization → Lock screen → Screen saver settings → confirm FluxScreensaver is selected → click Settings… to open its own configuration panel.

records.name: Turn on Context View · Screensaver (Flux Refocus)
records.purpose: In the panel's Tabatha section, under Context View, toggle "Show Tabatha Context View." On your primary display this replaces the flip clock with the live Context View; other displays keep the flip clock, and it falls back to the flip clock automatically if Tabatha can't be reached.

records.name: Sign in · Screensaver (Flux Refocus)
records.purpose: Under Account, click "Sign in to Tabatha…" — a one-time sign-in window. Once you're signed in, the Context View reuses that session automatically from then on.

records.name: Preview it · Screensaver (Flux Refocus)
records.purpose: Back in Windows' Screen Saver Settings, click Preview to test it full-screen, or just leave the screen idle for 5 minutes.

records.name: Install the Desktop Companion
records.purpose: A Windows tray app that turns time outside the browser into real activity data.
records.keywords: getting started Desktop Companion  Built · Windows

records.name: Download & install · Desktop Companion
records.purpose: Grab the Windows installer from the showcase site's Get the Desktop Companion panel, run it, and click through the SmartScreen warning above.

records.name: Find the tray icon · Desktop Companion
records.purpose: Tabby Desk lives in the Windows system tray once it's running — everything else here happens from that tray menu.

records.name: Pair it with the extension · Desktop Companion
records.purpose: Tray → 🔗 Pair Extension (copy token) copies a token; paste it into the browser extension's Settings → 🖥️ Desktop Activity → Companion Pairing → Save & Pair.

records.name: Clock in from the tray · Desktop Companion
records.purpose: Tray → 🕐 Clock In starts your shift from the desktop — the same shift the extension and Sidecar see, on the same clock.

records.name: Try Desk View · Desktop Companion
records.purpose: Tray → 🖥 Desk View opens a standing Context View window; toggle 📌 Desk View Always on Top to keep it pinned above other windows. It's the closest thing this family has to a screensaver — leave it running on a second monitor.

records.name: Leave it running on login · Desktop Companion
records.purpose: Tray → ✅ Start on Login is on by default; toggle it off there if you'd rather launch Tabby Desk manually.

records.name: Build & pair Tabby Watch
records.purpose: A Galaxy Watch 6 companion — focus ring, checkpoints, +5 extend. Sideload-only today.
records.keywords: getting started Tabby Watch  Sideload beta

records.name: Build it · Tabby Watch
records.purpose: Clone tabatha-watch, then run .\gradlew.bat :app:assembleDebug (needs JDK 17 and Android SDK 34). The APK lands at app\build\outputs\apk\debug\app-debug.apk.

records.name: Enable developer mode on the watch · Tabby Watch
records.purpose: Settings → About watch → Software → tap Software version 7 times.

records.name: Enable Wi-Fi debugging · Tabby Watch
records.purpose: Settings → Developer options → turn on ADB debugging and Debug over Wi-Fi; note the IP:port it shows.

records.name: Install it · Tabby Watch
records.purpose: From your PC: adb connect <watch-ip>:<port>, then adb -s <watch-ip>:<port> install -r app\build\outputs\apk\debug\app-debug.apk.

records.name: Pair it · Tabby Watch
records.purpose: Open Tabby Watch — it runs in preview mode until paired. See Pairing devices for the 6-digit code flow.

records.name: Add the Tile and complication · Tabby Watch
records.purpose: From the watch face's own customization menu, so your focus and remaining time show without opening the app.

records.name: Focus & intents
records.purpose: Start an intent, resolve it, and branch off a sub-intent without losing the parent.
records.keywords: how do i Gatekeeper · FocusInput · sub-intents · resolving

records.name: From a new tab (Extension) · Focus & intents
records.purpose: The Gatekeeper overlay asks "Why are you here?" Type into What are you working on? and press Continue for a real focus — or pick ⚔️ Side Quest, 🍬 Sugar Box, 🅿️ Park, or 🔖 Later for anything less than one.

records.name: From the dashboard or sidebar (Extension) · Focus & intents
records.purpose: Type into What are you focusing on? and press Set Focus (dashboard) or ▶ (sidebar).

records.name: From your phone (Sidecar) · Focus & intents
records.purpose: Focus tab → the "🎯 Set focus" card → type into What are you focusing on? (or tap the mic to speak it) → ▶ Set focus.

records.name: Mark it done · Focus & intents
records.purpose: Extension: ✓ Resolved on the FocusBar — on the dashboard, the sidebar, or a queue row. Sidecar: ✓ Resolve on the active-focus card, or the ✓ icon on a queue or sub-intent row.

records.name: Branch off the current focus · Focus & intents
records.purpose: Extension: 📌 Sub-focus on the FocusBar → type a name → + Add. Sidecar: the 📌 Sub-intent toggle on the Focus tab → type into Sub-intent under this focus… → Add sub-intent.

records.name: Work the list · Focus & intents
records.purpose: Sub-intents show under their parent with ▶ (switch to it) and ✓ (resolve it) — the parent focus's clock keeps running underneath.

records.name: Timers, extensions & backburner
records.purpose: Pause with a note, add +5 minutes, or put something aside without losing it.
records.keywords: how do i Pause/resume · +5m · timer expired · backburner · snooze

records.name: Extension · Timers, extensions & backburner
records.purpose: The InBar's ⏸ button opens "⏸ Where did you leave off?" — leave a note and press Pause. The page turns into a sticky note (📌 Paused) with ✏️ Edit Note and ▶ Resume buttons.

records.name: Sidecar · Timers, extensions & backburner
records.purpose: ⏸ Pause / ▶ Resume on the active-focus card.

records.name: Watch · Timers, extensions & backburner
records.purpose: The same chip toggles between ⏸ Pause and ▶ Resume.

records.name: Any surface · Timers, extensions & backburner
records.purpose: +5m on the extension's FocusBar, the Sidecar's active-focus card, or the watch's ＋5 min chip all add five minutes to the running timer.

records.name: The Focus Timer Expired overlay · Timers, extensions & backburner
records.purpose: Offers ⏱️ Snooze (pick your own minutes), 🍳 Let Me Cook, 🔄 Switch, ⏸ Pause, ☕ Break, ✅ Complete, and 📝 Note. There's no equivalent overlay on Sidecar or Watch today — just extend or pause manually there.
records.keywords: When the timer runs out (Extension only)

records.name: Put something aside (Extension) · Timers, extensions & backburner
records.purpose: InBar's 🔥 button opens "🔥 Backburner: '{focus}'" — set a Duration, say what you're waiting for, note what you'll work on instead, then Backburner.

records.name: Put something aside (Sidecar) · Timers, extensions & backburner
records.purpose: 🔥 Backburner button on the active-focus card. The Backburner list shows each item with ▶ (resume), ⏰ (snooze 10 minutes), and ✕ (dismiss).

records.name: Snooze · Timers, extensions & backburner
records.purpose: Sidecar's backburner rows snooze in a fixed 10-minute step. The extension's Focus Timer Expired overlay lets you snooze by any number of minutes you type in.

records.name: Checkpoints & voice
records.purpose: Log progress on a running focus, or just say it out loud instead of typing.
records.keywords: how do i Checkpoint notes · progress levels · voice capture · proactive check-ins

records.name: Extension · Checkpoints & voice
records.purpose: Open the checkpoint prompt from the InBar, the FocusBar, or the sidebar — it asks "What have you accomplished since your last checkpoint?", then "Submit with progress level:" — choose 😐 None, 📈 Little, 🚀 A Lot, 🏁 Almost Done, or 🚧 Stuck. If the InBar nudges you first, you can also ⏰ Snooze 5 min or Skip this time.

records.name: Sidecar · Checkpoints & voice
records.purpose: The 📋 Checkpoint toggle on the Focus tab opens What have you done since the last checkpoint? (with a mic button) and the same five-level picker: 😐 None, 📈 Little, 🚀 A lot, 🏁 Almost, 🚧 Stuck.

records.name: Speak instead of typing · Checkpoints & voice
records.purpose: Tap the mic next to any text field — setting a focus, adding a checkpoint — and it fills the field from what you say.

records.name: Turn it on · Checkpoints & voice
records.purpose: Settings → "Voice check-ins" card → "Proactive spoken check-ins" (off by default).

records.name: What happens · Checkpoints & voice
records.purpose: Go quiet mid-focus and Tabby asks out loud, "How's {focus} going?" Tap 🎙 Check in to answer any time, or just respond when it asks — try "extend 10 minutes," "pause," "resume," "done," or just describe your progress.

records.name: Confirmation · Checkpoints & voice
records.purpose: What you say applies with a visible confirmation strip (e.g. "✓ Paused") and an Undo link — extends don't get Undo; use ✏️ Update focus instead if one was wrong.

records.name: Quiet hours · Checkpoints & voice
records.purpose: Shared with nudges — Settings → "Work schedule & nudges" → Quiet hours start / end.

records.name: What it is · Checkpoints & voice
records.purpose: Settings → "Chaperone voice" → "Personality interrupts", with its own quiet hours (Off / 10pm–8am / 9pm–9am). This is what speaks a nudge on a paired Context View screen if you wander off — see Phone Focus Mode & the red screen.

records.name: Tasks & Asana sync
records.purpose: Keep a task list, or connect Asana from your phone so tasks show up everywhere.
records.keywords: how do i Create a task · connect Asana (PAT) · start a task as a focus

records.name: Extension · Tasks & Asana sync
records.purpose: New Tab Dashboard → Tasks → + New Task → fill it in → Create.

records.name: Sidecar · Tasks & Asana sync
records.purpose: The Tasks tab.

records.name: Get a Personal Access Token · Tasks & Asana sync
records.purpose: In Asana: Settings → Apps → Developer apps → Personal access tokens — create one and copy it.

records.name: Paste it into Sidecar · Tasks & Asana sync
records.purpose: Settings → "Task sync" card → paste it into Paste your Asana personal access token → Connect Asana. The token is stored encrypted server-side and never shown again; once connected the card reads "Asana — connected" with a Sync now button.

records.name: Why the phone · Tasks & Asana sync
records.purpose: The Chrome extension has no PAT field by design — the token never ships inside the (world-readable) extension code. Connect once on Sidecar and the synced tasks, subtasks, and blocked badges appear wherever your account is signed in.

records.name: Extension · Tasks & Asana sync
records.purpose: The 🎯 icon on a task ("Start a focus from this task").
records.keywords: Start a task as a focus

records.name: Sidecar · Tasks & Asana sync
records.purpose: The ▶ on a task row.
records.keywords: Start a task as a focus

records.name: Work shifts & clock
records.purpose: Clock in, take a break, and see the same shift no matter which device you're on.
records.keywords: how do i Clock in/out · breaks · cross-device shifts · shift history

records.name: Extension · Work shifts & clock
records.purpose: Home dashboard's Shift Controls → ▶ Clock In / ⏹ Clock Out (the sidebar has the same as compact icon buttons).

records.name: Sidecar · Work shifts & clock
records.purpose: Clock tab → ▶ Clock in / ⏹ Clock out; status reads 🟢 Clocked in, ☕ On break, or ⚪ Clocked out under "Your shift."

records.name: Desktop Companion · Work shifts & clock
records.purpose: Tray → 🕐 Clock In.

records.name: Toggle a break · Work shifts & clock
records.purpose: Extension: ☕ Break / ▶ Resume next to the clock buttons. Sidecar: the same toggle on the Clock tab.

records.name: One shift, seen everywhere · Work shifts & clock
records.purpose: Clocking in on one device is the same shift on all of them. Sidecar's Clock tab has an "Also on the clock" section for other active installs — clock actions sync to your account, and the desktop reflects them on its next sync.

records.name: Extension · Work shifts & clock
records.purpose: ⏱️ Shifts button opens Work Shifts — daily hours, peak hours, break patterns, and live stints across installs.

records.name: Sidecar · Work shifts & clock
records.purpose: The "Recent shifts" list on the Clock tab.

records.name: Phone Focus Mode & the red screen
records.purpose: Wander off your phone mid-focus and a paired big screen turns red.
records.keywords: how do i Phone-away detection · the red alert · fade speed

records.name: Turn it on · Phone Focus Mode & the red screen
records.purpose: Sidecar → Focus tab → the 📵 Phone Focus Mode switch. Off, it just nudges you if you navigate away; on, it warns: "put your phone down — wander off and your big screen goes red."

records.name: What triggers it · Phone Focus Mode & the red screen
records.purpose: Leaving or backgrounding the Sidecar browser tab — detected the moment it loses visibility — while Phone Focus Mode is on and a focus is active.

records.name: What happens on your phone · Phone Focus Mode & the red screen
records.purpose: A local notification: "👀 Back to it" — "You stepped away from Tabatha while in Focus Mode. Eyes back on the task 👇"

records.name: What happens on the big screen · Phone Focus Mode & the red screen
records.purpose: If a paired Context View is showing, it turns into a red "📵 Put the phone down" screen: "You stepped away from focus — back to it."

records.name: Control how fast it turns red · Phone Focus Mode & the red screen
records.purpose: Extension Settings → 📺 Context View → "Phone-away fade" → Slow fade (7 seconds) or Immediate. Sidecar's own Settings screen has an equivalent "Immediate phone-away alert" switch, in case the phone is what's handy.

records.name: Pairing devices
records.purpose: Link a Galaxy Watch or the desktop companion to your Tabatha account.
records.keywords: how do i Watch pairing (6-digit code) · Companion pairing (token)

records.name: Get a code, on your phone · Pairing devices
records.purpose: Sidecar → Settings → ⌚ Pair a watch → Get pairing code. It shows a 6-digit code, valid 5 minutes, that works once.

records.name: Enter it, on the watch · Pairing devices
records.purpose: Open Tabby Watch → tap "⌚ Pair with Tabatha" (or "⌚ Re-pair watch" if you've paired before) → enter the 6 digits. It shows "Pairing…" then "Paired ✓."

records.name: If it fails · Pairing devices
records.purpose: "Code invalid or expired — mint a fresh one on your phone" just means the 5 minutes ran out, or it was already used — get a new code and try again.

records.name: Copy a token, on the desktop · Pairing devices
records.purpose: Tabby Desk's tray menu → 🔗 Pair Extension (copy token) copies a pairing token to your clipboard.

records.name: Paste it, in the browser · Pairing devices
records.purpose: Extension Settings → 🖥️ Desktop Activity → Companion Pairing → paste it into Paste pairing token → Save & Pair. You'll see "✓ Token saved — reconnecting to the companion."

records.name: Is it required? · Pairing devices
records.purpose: No — it's a security-hardening step, not a hard requirement. An unpaired companion still connects in open mode; pair it if you want the link authenticated.

records.name: Settings & customization
records.purpose: Tune Context View, install the apps, and set up a standing screen.
records.keywords: how do i Context View settings · install the PWA · install the companion · Desk View

records.name: Where · Settings & customization
records.purpose: Extension Settings → 📺 Context View (sign in first, under Sync & Account — an anonymous profile has nothing to save to).

records.name: What you can change · Settings & customization
records.purpose: Toggle Day countdown, Up next, Timeline, and Show last checkpoint; pick Phone-away fade (Slow fade / Immediate); set "Day resets at (hr)" — the hour (0–23) the day countdown counts down to.

records.name: When it applies · Settings & customization
records.purpose: Changes reach any paired big screen right away — no separate sync step.

records.name: Tabby Sidecar (PWA) · Settings & customization
records.purpose: On your phone: Settings → Account → 📲 Install Tabby (Android/desktop Chrome), or Share → Add to Home Screen on iOS.

records.name: Desktop Companion · Settings & customization
records.purpose: See Install the Desktop Companion — it's an unsigned installer, so expect a SmartScreen prompt on first run.

records.name: Desk View (from the Desktop Companion) · Settings & customization
records.purpose: Desktop Companion tray → 🖥 Desk View opens Context View in its own window; 📌 Desk View Always on Top keeps it pinned above everything else. Good for a second monitor you glance at all day.

records.name: An actual screensaver (Flux Refocus, optional) · Settings & customization
records.purpose: A separate app — Flux Refocus's screensaver clock — can show the same Context View automatically whenever a Windows machine goes idle, with a flip-clock fallback. It's a different repo with no installer yet; see Screensaver (Flux Refocus).

records.name: Feedback & troubleshooting
records.purpose: Report a bug, ask for something new, or figure out why two devices disagree.
records.keywords: how do i Send feedback (extension, Sidecar) · sync mismatches

records.name: From the extension · Feedback & troubleshooting
records.purpose: Toolbar popup → 💬 Send feedback → choose 🐛 Bug or 💡 Idea → describe it → Send. You'll see "✓ Thanks!", or a retry message if it couldn't send.

records.name: From Tabby Sidecar · Feedback & troubleshooting
records.purpose: Settings → 💬 Feedback & bug report → 🐛 Bug or 💡 Feature request → describe it → Send feedback. Offline, it's saved locally with "📥 Saved locally — we'll send it once the feedback pipeline is live" and a Retry now button once you're back online.

records.name: About a specific screenshot on this site · Feedback & troubleshooting
records.purpose: Every card on the Show and Tell component gallery has a 🐞 Bug / ✨ Request pair that appears on hover.

records.name: Check sign-in first · Feedback & troubleshooting
records.purpose: Settings → ☁️ Sync & Account on each device. Most cross-device weirdness — a focus that "didn't follow" you, a task that isn't showing up — traces back to being signed into different accounts, or not signed in at all, on one of them.
